dependencies: Independent agents and brokers for distribution
Third-party service providers for technology and business processes
Lloyd's of London for international licenses
State guaranty funds and residual market pools
risks: Reinsurers may not pay reinsurance recoverables or may not pay on a timely basis
Credit risk with respect to reinsurers if they fail to pay claims
Inability to renew or replace existing reinsurance coverage
Market conditions may make reinsurance more costly or unavailable
Dependence on third-party vendors and service providers for technology systems
Cybersecurity breaches of information technology systems of vendors and third parties
Credit risk relating to policyholders, independent agents and brokers
Reliance on independent pricing services for investment valuations

regulatory_risks: Extensive governmental regulation and supervision in U.S. and foreign jurisdictions
State insurance departments conduct periodic examinations and require annual reports
Holding company statutes require prior regulatory approval for acquisitions over 10% of voting securities
NAIC model holding company act requires group capital calculation filing
Cybersecurity regulations including New York NYDFS cybersecurity regulation
NAIC Insurance Data Security Model Law adopted by several states
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA)
GDPR and U.K. GDPR for data protection in EU and U.K.
NAIC Model Bulletin on Use of Artificial Intelligence Systems by Insurers
Colorado law prohibiting unfair discrimination in use of external consumer data and AI
New York Insurance Circular Letter 7 on AI and external consumer data
Risk-Based Capital (RBC) requirements by NAIC
Insurance Regulatory Information System (IRIS) ratios monitored by NAIC
State guaranty fund assessments for insolvent insurers
Mandatory shared market or pooling arrangements for certain insurance types
Dividend restrictions on insurance subsidiaries requiring regulatory approval
Terrorism Risk Insurance Program Reauthorization Act of 2019 (TRIPRA)
Dodd-Frank Act establishing FIO and FSOC with potential federal oversight
Covered Agreements with EU and U.K. on reinsurance collateral requirements
Potential designation as systemically important financial institution (non-bank SIFI)
Climate change regulatory requirements including NYDFS circular letter
EU Solvency II regime for capital adequacy and risk management
U.K. Solvency U.K. regime diverging from EU Solvency II
IAIS global framework for supervision of IAIGs including ICS capital standard
Bermuda Corporate Income Tax Act 2023 imposing 15% tax effective 2025
NAIC and state regulators focus on diversity and corporate governance
Trade practices regulations governing marketing, sales, claims management
Investment regulations prescribing kind, quality and concentration of investments
Rate and form regulation for certain insurance businesses
Excess and surplus lines regulation and reporting requirements
Federal financial services modernization legislation
Potential federal regulation of insurance including optional federal charter proposals
International insurance regulatory regimes in Mexico, Australia, Canada, Europe, South America, Southeast Asia

sanction_risks: Potential impact of sanctions on international operations
Exposure to countries subject to U.S. or international sanctions
